    • Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the speeding EXSJT

`knitr::kable()` wrapper with stripped leading zeros

Source: R/f0.R
kable0.Rd

Thin wrapper that pre-processes a data frame via df_f0 before handing it to knitr::kable(). Used in funfield vignettes to keep tabular output consistent with the house style: decimals less than one render as .123 rather than 0.123.

Usage

kable0(x, digits = 3, keep_sign = FALSE, align = NULL, ...)

Arguments

x

A data frame or anything df_f0 can format (numeric columns become character with stripped leading zeros).

digits

Number of decimal places (default 3). Passed to df_f0(), not to knitr::kable().

keep_sign

Logical; if TRUE, prepend "+" for non-negative non-zero values. Default FALSE.

...

Additional arguments passed to knitr::kable() (e.g., caption, col.names, format).

Value

A knitr_kable object.

On this page
